programmes.show_description: ...Experiment: Jess and Ben explore a non-monogamous lifestyle in the hot tub with Olivia and Gage, where Jess faces a big fear. And Kirsty and Tom consider attending a huge orgy.
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. With Britain at war, Emma takes a huge gamble to stay afloat. But it could cost her far more than her business. (Ep7/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. As the war rages on, Emma battles to keep her empire afloat, but would she really get into bed with the Fairleys? (Ep8/8)
A musical waiter, a Boris-loving singleton and an '80s-obsessed romantic - this time First Dates serves up chaos over a spilled drink, a bit of chemistry and a few curveballs.
Kirstie Allsopp puts celebs to the test in crafting challenges. Chris Hollins and Laura Hamilton craft colourful paper gift bags.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Mother and daughter Linda and Kelly host first at The Old Times Guest House in Ipswich. They take their guests go-karting, but Kelly cuts corners with hot drink orders at breakfast.
The second visit is to The Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames, where owner Simon and manager Pierre offer their guests a stylish stay and a game of real tennis at Hampton Court Palace.
The third visit is to the Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, where owner Jo takes her guests paintballing and to a floating restaurant. But there are suspicions of game-playing.
Pat and Nigel pride themselves on their award-winning breakfast at their B&B in Seaton, Devon. But will an afternoon spent town-crying entertain their guests?
With all the visits over, the B&Bers come together to confront their rivals and discover how much they've been paid, before the winner is announced.
Emma Louise and Liv host at their 527-year-old pub in Kent, where spooky, spectral tales and a chaotic bout of drag bingo and karaoke await the B&Bers.
At Ami and Mark's plush glampsite in Kent, the B&Bers are hailing their huts. But alarm bells are ringing for one pair in their bell tent.
Saban hosts at his 700-year-old house in Maidstone, where the magnificent historic vibes aren't enough to stop evening drinks turning terrifically tense...
Mark and Anne host at their Margate townhouse, where the atmosphere turns icy over ice cream at the beach thanks to one guest who just can't forget about his own feedback.
After a feisty week in Kent - when some B&Bers couldn't even wait until payment day to get their gripes off their chest - it is now actually payment day.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In North Wales, Henry Cole finds an antique cannon in Sue and Josh's storage shed, while Simon O'Brien discovers a croquet set in need of restoration.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole find a gramophone and restyle a stowaway bed. Simon accidentally puts his foot in it with a drum, while Henry decides that he's a silver surfer.
Gary and Alison are looking for a rural holiday home in the Haute-Vienne area of central France. Can Laura Hamilton find them the perfect place for their budget of £100k?
Sharon and Brendon want to buy a holiday home beside beautiful Lake Garda, but they each have very different priorities... Can Lucy Alexander fulfil their dreams?
Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman shows them five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget. Are any of them perfect?
Jaazzmina and Mark want to create a remote Spanish smallholding and wellness retreat in Almería Province. Can Sara Damergi find the right spot for £100,000?
Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. It's a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?
This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
What do Britain's sharpest armchair critics think of Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Race Across the World, The Piano, I Kissed a Boy and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen?
...Diamond Heist: In 2015, a gang of ageing criminals pulled off a multi-million heist in London's Hatton Garden diamond district. What happened next? And where are the stolen millions?
(2006) Family action-thriller with Alex Pettyfer and Mickey Rourke. British intelligence recruit a schoolboy to take on the man who killed his dad and is planning to unleash a deadly virus.
...Champions 2025: They've already won Greg's golden noggin. But which series supremo can go one better? Andy Zaltzman, John Robins, Maisie Adam, Mathew Baynton and Sam Campbell do battle.
A security guard manages a violent incident with a patient. Jack, who's 33, is rushed into A&E after falling from a horse. And seven-year-old Lyla has a finger infection.

programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at lofty ambitions in Walthamstow in East London, where the Clayton-Fisher family totally transformed their dark and dingy attic.

Teleshopping.
(2011) Family comedy. Jim Carrey stars as a work-obsessed New York property developer whose life is turned upside down when he receives a crate containing six live penguins.
(2010) Family fantasy comedy. Dwayne Johnson stars as a tough ice hockey player who is transformed into a real-life fairy as punishment for telling a child that tooth fairies don't exist.
Tony and Julie from Manchester want to buy their first property together on Italy's beautiful Trabocchi coast. Danni Menzies shows them five options for their £150,000 budget.
Jaazzmina and Mark want to create a remote Spanish smallholding and wellness retreat in Almería Province. Can Sara Damergi find the right spot for £100,000?
We catch up with Carole and Alan, who needed a home with lots of land and holiday let potential in Somerset or Devon. And did Ros and Hannah find the perfect first home in London?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back on Sarah and Ian Renard's two-bed rural renovation in Brixworth that didn't compromise on character.
Yeovil. PR manager Emmy hopes to impress her guests with locally sourced Somerset produce. But she has a disaster with her hollandaise sauce. (Ep1/5)
Yeovil. Radio DJ Steve serves up a cross between comfort food and fine dining. But he feels the pressure, and an unwanted ingredient ends up on one guest's plate. (Ep2/5)
Yeovil. Diminutive forklift driver Mel plans an exotic menu, including her version of duck vindaloo, and surprises her guests by breaking into song. (Ep3/5)
Yeovil. Business owner and muscleman Alan hopes to create a Zen evening. But with rotten beans, tofu cheesecake and soggy noodles on the menu, it could be a recipe for disaster... (Ep4/5)
Yeovil. Car dealer Andy is confident that his fine dining and swanky home will give his night the edge. But Mel isn't afraid to tell him exactly what she thinks of his fancy food. (Ep5/5)
...Secrets Unwrapped: A fascinating look inside the Willy Wonka world of Cadbury across a make-or-break year, as they try to reduce the sugar in Dairy Milk and launch a Creme Egg campaign.

programmes.show_name: Help! I Bought It at Auction...
programmes.show_description: ...with Sarah Beeny: In Nottinghamshire, Laura turns a century-old bungalow with ivy growing under the wallpaper into a dream home. In Stoke-on-Trent, Tariq takes on a former library. (Ep2)
Artist Sarah returns to her roots in the Durham Dales to build a cannabis-based home. But costs go through the roof. Can she get her hempcrete hub of a home over the line?
(2022) It's Predator, 1719 style. Amber Midthunder stars as a Comanche hunter who must save her tribe from an extraterrestrial threat in this action prequel. If it bleeds, you can kill it.
